Beauty and the Beast (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $504,014,165Details
International Box Office $755,747,521
Worldwide Box Office $1,259,761,686
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $29,045,412
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $60,000,971
Total $89,046,383
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The fantastic journey of Belle, a bright, beautiful and independent young woman who is taken prisoner by a Beast in his castle. Despite her fears, she befriends the castle’s enchanted staff and learns to look beyond the Beast’s hideous exterior and realize the kind heart of the true Prince within.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$174,750,616 (34.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.88 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$160,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,210 opening theaters/4,210 max. theaters, 8.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $672,018,885

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 17th, 2017 (Wide) by Walt Disney
March 17th, 2017 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: March 16th, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
March 16th, 2017 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
March 16th, 2017 (Wide) (China)
March 16th, 2017 (Wide), released as Красавицата и звяра (Bulgaria)
March 17th, 2017 (Wide) (Taiwan)
Video Release: June 6th, 2017 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some action violence, peril and frightening images.
(Rating bulletin 2445 (Cert #50712), 10/5/2016)
Running Time: 123 minutes
Franchise: Beauty and the Beast
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animated Inanimate Objects, Live Action Remake, Motion Capture Performance, Prologue, Remake, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (country): England, United Kingdom
Filming location (production facility): Shepperton Studios, Surrey, England
Filming location (state or county): Surrey, England
Geographical setting: Paris
Plot point: Curses, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Hostage, Interspecies Romance, Non-Chronological, Romance, Royalty, Unrequited Love, Witches, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: Bubonic Plague / Black Death
Subgenre: Family Musical
Source:Based on Folk Tale/Legend/Fairytale
Genre:Musical
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, Mandeville Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The fantastic journey of Belle, a bright, beautiful and independent young woman who is taken prisoner by a Beast in his castle. Despite her fears, she befriends the castle’s enchanted staff and learns to look beyond the Beast’s hideous exterior and realize the kind heart of the true Prince within.
